scope: Flash S=4096 split-compressed sparse MLA backward primitive profile on one MI350.

decision.read: The split-compressed Triton-softmax sparse MLA backward path is correctness-clean and about 10.2% faster than the retained standalone backward probe at Flash S=4096. It does not close the full attention gap by itself; the modeled primitive sum remains about 5.04 ms.
next_kernel_focus: Fusing the CSA compressed-score/grad/value-add sequence is the largest local primitive family at about 1.93 ms.
The split softmax backward kernel is still about 1.25 ms and remains a fusion or specialization target.
Sparse window-side q/selected accumulation is about 1.19 ms and is the next meaningful local family after CSA and softmax.
HCA, window reduction, and sink-gradient reduction are small; do not prioritize them first.
decision.target_context: The one-MI350 layer target is 24.9 ms full fwd+bwd+grads; current Flash split full-layer evidence remains above target, so this is a useful sub-ms step rather than the final attention solution.

run_control.command: RUN_TAG=sparse_mla_split_primitive_flash_s4096_retry_20260531 SPARSE_MLA_BWD_VARIANT=split-compressed-triton-softmax SPARSE_MLA_BWD_ITERS=5 SPARSE_MLA_BWD_PRIMITIVE_PROFILE=true SPARSE_MLA_BWD_CHECK_VARIANTS=retained WAIT_IDLE_SECONDS=300 bash experiments/2026-05-27-deepseek-v4-amd-pretrain/tools/dsv4_layer_microbench/run_retained_mi350_suite.sh probe-sparse-mla-backward-4096
